channel.title: Comments for page "Document OWi"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Document OWi"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A "white human"? Seems a bit odd for a Foundation document. SCiP 1000's shouldn't be referred to so casually. And really, this is an awfully low-impact piece for a 001 claimant. Just a list of things that are different in small, very mildly interesting weight.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's been a while since this log was added to so I added an entry for 1000

item.title: Re: Eastern Samothrace
item.content:encoded:
Well, thanks for keeping the original data, at least.

That might or might be the data restricted only to O5-1, but I suppose it is up to one's interpretation.

item.title: Re: Eastern Samothrace
item.content:encoded:
P.S. IIRC, ORIA does believe that Eastern Samothrace is partially in reality.

That's exactly why I added that. :P

Well, thanks for keeping the original data, at least.

item.title: Eastern Samothrace
item.content:encoded:
Although I effectively changed the crosslink to 1173 wholesale, I will admit that I find the take here to be interesting.

However, I am more concerned with the implication of diminishing 1173's story. I mean, 1173's story has even the O5 unsure of the truth. Since this 001 proposal and log are presented as knowledge privy to the O5, many crosslinks to SCP-1173 risk that diminishment. I myself fell into that trap before I removed the reference (I wrote that Samothrace happened in the previous reality, which was rectonned away).

It is my fault for not accounting for that, but this gave me impetus to do something. Keeping the truth within O5-1 should retain the mystery (the only O5 not involved in 1173's article).

Nonetheless, I do think the story presented here can be given more life than a few sentences (i.e. a full article).

+ show block
– hide block

Phenomenon: The Islamic Republic of Eastern Skanta

Description: A political body formed from the refugees from the ruins of Skanta, a small town near London, arrived to a new land to escape the war called the Islamic Republic of Eastern Skanta. The Republic was well known for its memetic anomalies, which they would use to assist Her Majesty's Foundation for the Secure Containment of the Paranormal.

Analogue in Current Reality: According to SCP-████, the Islamic Republic of Eastern Skanta contracted an anti-memetic virus, which began to destroy the concept of the Republic itself. It is said that the Council that governed the Republic enacted an emergency protocol to change the republic's name from every single recorded item referencing them, thus making them immune from further metaphysical manipulation. However, due to the outbreak, The new Republic now only exists partially within the current reality.

P.S. IIRC, ORIA does believe that Eastern Samothrace is partially in reality.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Mkay, that's better. Thanks for that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I made some edits to that one:

Analogue in Current Reality: According to the confiscated text [REDACTED], the Cicada Lord was supposedly furious at the Preacher sometime during his servitude. He then shed the man's skin, and allowed his spawn to manifest in the empty skin. The puppet is said to wander around North America, giving speeches of "hope" to the participants of family gatherings.

I think I would rather make it feel like it comes from a source (which can suggest some degree of doubt). I feel that if it is outright stated, it might imply to be a "truth" (which might work better as an independent article).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wish I had a better handle on the whole alternate histories idea so I could contribute!

Well, my tastes for alternate history is largely inspired by deviantart's RvBOMally and RoyalPsycho (whose works I like lurking).

I would say that the idea of a global war in the 19th century using supernatural objects is inspired from the many works of the two.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: added SCP-2354

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm actually having fun with this….

Added Cousin Johnny to the history hi-jinks. :3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This document gives me anything but an owie :D This is some good stuff! I wish I had a better handle on the whole alternate histories idea so I could contribute!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I suppose it would have been massively altered along the way from a failed prototype

Correct! However, I didn't really clarify it well enough. Ill edit it to clarify it.

A "gender-bent" MC&D… Nothing really off-putting, but that was unexpected.

Glad that stood out to you. :]

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hmm… 2305 is rather unexpected, but it does meet rule no. 1.

Since SCP-2305 is specifically designated to fuck with the Foundation, I suppose it would have been massively altered along the way from a failed prototype OR the Foundation's creation was already determined from the moment the failed prototype exists OR something else.

But those are my musings from the contribution.

Marry, Catherine, and Duke's trade goods

A "gender-bent" MC&D… Nothing really off-putting, but that was unexpected.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eh, fuck it. I threw my hat into the ring. Hope ya like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hurray for the first contribution! I suppose it is refreshing to see the so-called "Sick Man of Europe" to be not so sick.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Even though I usually don't like collaborations, this is a really cool idea and I've added a log to get the ball rolling.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hey, this is brilliant.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: More precisely, a collab page centred on an event related to an 001 proposal.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: An SCP-001 collab page? +all the 1s.

item.title: Rules of Collab Supplement
item.content:encoded:
Since the History Contest is over, I suppose this can be unveiled for all.

1. Whatever anomaly you are using should be something that can feasibly exist in the 19th century. If you are using an SCP whose article explicitly states that it exists OR becomes anomalous only during the 20th century onwards, no go. If the era of creation is not explicitly stated, go for it.

2. Since the Foundation was not a thing during Occult War i, do give any SCPs you're using another name when referring to them in Phenomenon and/or Description.

3. You are free to use anomalies outside of SCPs. You can totally reference anomalies solely mentioned in Tales or GoI articles, so long they satisfy Rule 1.

4. History repeats itself. Try to write such that things are more or less similar (to a very uncanny level) in the previous and current realities.

5. Group the phenomena according to region.

6. The context here is ultimately a war, so let that be a framing device for how you can write your entries.

7. Note that the in-universe writers of these entries are the original O5 Council in Wrong Proposal. The writing is ultimately from their POV. Also, at the time of Occult War i, they were still in their original organisations.

As with every collab page, this is subject to pruning every now and then.

This is my first collaboration page, so let us collaborate.
